audience_definition: IN-AUDIENCE = report COMPILER who builds a recurring team status from a tracker (PM, EM, data/ops analyst, team lead, founder-operator, marketing/demand-gen LEAD). NON-FIT = IC who writes only their own bullets, solo/freelance designer, anyone not compiling a recurring team status.

personas.name: Priya
personas.stack: terminal + neovim, GitHub, Kubernetes dashboards, Datadog, Linear, Slack
personas.temperament: skeptical of new tools; hates signups; will inspect network tab if suspicious; abandons anything slower than a CLI
personas.motivation: Weak fit: as an IC she writes only her own standup bullets straight in Slack; she doesn't compile a team-wide weekly status from a tracker. She might glance at it if a manager shared it, but it solves no recurring job of hers — honest low motivation.

personas.name: Marcus
personas.stack: VS Code, GitHub, Vercel, Figma dev mode, Storybook, Discord/Slack
personas.temperament: enthusiastic early adopter; shares tools in team Slack when impressed; notices janky CSS instantly
personas.motivation: Weak fit as a report job: he reports up, not down — he doesn't aggregate a team status. But as an early adopter he might try it on a GitHub issues export out of curiosity and share it if it's slick. No recurring need of his own.

personas.name: Wen
personas.stack: BigQuery/SQL, Google Sheets, Looker Studio, dbt, GA4, Slack
personas.temperament: lives in data hygiene; distrusts tools that transform data invisibly; wants CSV in/out everywhere
personas.motivation: Builds recurring stakeholder progress reports and lives in CSV. She'd drop a Jira/Asana export to auto-categorize work into a weekly status for marketing-ops stakeholders, but will scrutinize whether the tool silently mis-buckets rows.

personas.name: Tomás
personas.role: Operations analyst at a mid-size company
personas.stack: Excel (power user), Tableau, Jira, Teams, SharePoint
personas.temperament: pragmatic; IT blocks installs so browser tools appeal; wary of pasting company data into random sites
personas.motivation: Compiles weekly progress reports for stakeholders from Jira exports today by hand in Excel. A no-install, client-side tool that turns a Jira CSV into a Shipped/In-Progress/Blocked status is exactly his recurring chore — but he must trust nothing leaves the browser.

personas.name: Dana
personas.device: MacBook in cafes; phone between meetings
personas.stack: HubSpot, GA4, Notion, Canva, LinkedIn Campaign Manager, Google Ads
personas.temperament: ruthless about time; if value isn't obvious in one scroll she's gone; screenshots tools she likes for the team channel
personas.motivation: As a demand-gen lead she owes a weekly status on campaign/project progress and runs a small team in Asana. She'd use this to turn an Asana export into a stakeholder update — but only if the value lands in one scroll.

personas.name: Jules
personas.stack: Notion, Buffer, Mailchimp, Figma (consuming), X/LinkedIn/Mastodon, Discord
personas.temperament: posts constantly, juggles platform quirks daily; allergic to anything requiring a login for a small job
personas.motivation: Weak fit: tracks content in Notion/Buffer, not a Jira/Linear issue tracker, and reports informally. No recurring CSV-export-to-status job. Might try it once but it doesn't map to her workflow.

personas.name: Aisha
personas.stack: Figma, FigJam, Notion, Loom, Maze, Slack
personas.temperament: judges craft hard — spacing, copy tone, empty states; a clumsy UI loses her regardless of utility; advocates loudly for tools that feel considered
personas.motivation: Borderline: she contributes to status but doesn't own a recurring team-wide tracker digest. Primarily she'll judge craft — empty states, copy tone, the digest layout — and advocate only if it feels considered, not from her own recurring need.

personas.name: Rob
personas.stack: Figma, Illustrator, Photoshop, Google Drive, client Slacks/emails
personas.temperament: does repetitive asset-prep grunt work weekly; price-sensitive; compares everything to 'I could do this in Photoshop in 4 minutes'
personas.motivation: No fit: a solo freelance designer has no team tracker and no weekly team-status report to compile. He'd never land here in his real workflow — honest zero motivation.

personas.name: Elena
personas.device: laptop + phone, half the day in meetings
personas.stack: Google Calendar (wall-to-wall), Slack, Linear, Google Docs, Zoom, dashboards she skims
personas.temperament: 30-second patience budget; uses tools between meetings on her phone; anything requiring setup is dead to her; recommends tools that save her reports time
personas.motivation: Owes a weekly update on her 8 reports' progress and lives in Linear. Exporting a Linear CSV to instantly get a Shipped/In-Progress/Blocked digest grouped by assignee, carry-over flagged, would save her the worst part of her Friday — if it's truly 30 seconds.

personas.name: Sam
personas.stack: Notion, Sheets, Slack, Zoom, Asana, Amplitude (skims), endless browser tabs
personas.temperament: synthesizes and shares constantly — exports, links, screenshots; values anything that makes him look organized; mild tech confidence, won't debug anything
personas.motivation: Compiles a weekly stakeholder status from Asana every week and loves anything that makes him look organized. Dropping an Asana export to get a categorized, copy-to-Slack digest grouped by epic is squarely his recurring job — won't debug it though.
